LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAY-17-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT AUSTIN CARMICHAEL'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS, DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED WITH PREJUDICE. FIRST, DEFENDANT FAILED TO COMPLY WITH THE SAFE HARBOR PROVISION OF CCP 128.7(C)(1). SECOND, THE MOTION DOES NOT DESCRIBE IMPROPER, HARASSING, OR FRIVOLOUS CONDUCT WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 128.7(B). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING WITH A COVER LETTER STATING COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ROCHELLE C. EAST; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RCE)

MOTION To Amend Order Granting Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Judgment; Declarations
Off Calendar May-24-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with Case No. CGC-19-581523, Bank of America vs. Austin Carringnton Carmichael.

Appearance via Zoom:
Kurtiss Jacobs, Esq., Tel. 408.414.0369, appearing for plaintiff Bank of America;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT AUSTIN CARMICHAEL's Motion To Amend Order Granting Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Judgment.

The matter is not contested. No other appearance in this matter. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Austin Carrington Carmichael's motion to amend the order granting the motion to vacate and set aside the default judgment is denied as defective. Carmichael's motion fails to include a notice of motion stating the nature of the order being sought and the grounds for the issuance of the order. (CRC 3.1110(a).) Moreover, to the extent that Carmichael is seeking to add terms to an order that the court has already issued, his motion is denied with prejudice.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Park. (301/RCE)

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar May-24-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with Case No. CGC-19-581523, Bank of America vs. Austin Carringnton Carmichael.

Appearances via Zoom:
Kurtiss Jacobs, Esq., Tel. 408.414.0369, appearing for plaintiff Bank of America;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Hearing re: DEFENDANT AUSTIN CARMICHAEL'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES.

The matter is not contested. No other appearance in this matter. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Austin Carrington Carmichael's motion for attorney's fees is denied with prejudice. Carmichael cannot recover attorney's fees and costs as he is not an attorney. (Trope v. Katz (1995) 11 Cal.4th 275, 285.) "[I]t is clear that when it enacted section 1717 the Legislature did not intend to allow doctors, architects, painters, or any other nonattorneys to receive compensation for the valuable time they spend litigating a contract matter on their own behalf." (Id.)

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Park. (301/RCE)

Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar May-17-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. KURTISS JACOBS, Esq. of Hunt & Henriques, LLP (tel: 408-414-0369) for Plaintiff.
Defendant AUSTIN CARRINGTON CARMICHAEL, in pro per present (tel: 415-260-8130).

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT AUSTIN CARMICHAEL's Motion For Sanctions.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion for sanctions is denied with prejudice. First, defendant failed to comply with the safe harbor provision of CCP 128.7(c)(1). Second, the motion does not describe improper, harassing, or frivolous conduct within the meaning of CCP 128.7(b).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a cover letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RCE)

MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT AUSTIN CARMICHAEL's Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Judgment To The Claims In The Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is off calendar. This "motion to vacate" appears to be a pleading analogous to a cross-complaint. There is therefore nothing for the court to rule on. In any event, the "motion" was not timely filed and served per CCP 1005.
